text.title: Competitor Creative Scrape AI Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Competitor Creative Scrape AI` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each scraped creative MUST link to its source (Meta Ad Library URL / TikTok Creative Center URL / LinkedIn Sales Navigator URL) and timestamp. Anonymous creatives forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anonymous creatives cannot be re-verified or legally referenced; source-pinning is the audit-trail requirement.

rule.statement: Each creative MUST be classified along three axes: hook type (problem / proof / curiosity / FOMO), visual type (UGC / studio / animation / static), CTA type (download / signup / book-demo / shop). Unclassified creatives forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unclassified creatives cannot be A/B'd against; the three-axis taxonomy makes pattern extraction tractable.

rule.statement: Each new report MUST dedup against the previous 90 days of scraped creatives. Repeated creatives are noted as 'still live' but not re-recommended.
rule.rationale: Repeated recommendations cause CRO team to lose trust in the report; 90-day dedup keeps the inspiration list fresh.

rule.statement: Each weekly report MUST name the CRO owner who acts on the recommendations. Group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Group-owned reports get triaged by nobody; the named owner schedules the experiments within the cycle.

rule.statement: Where AI classifies hook/visual/CTA, the report MUST link to the AI prompt + model version used + sample evaluation. Bald AI judgments are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Un-evidenced AI claims drift over time as models change; evidence anchors the classification to a reproducible prompt.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
